claim.observation: ASCII strings extracted from the $DATA of NTFS inode 80517 (identified by fls/istat as Users/vibranium/AppData/Local/Temp/a.exe, a 9216-byte PE) include the embedded PDB path c:\code\httppump\inner\objchk_wxp_x86\i386\i.pdb and the imported APIs WriteProcessMemory, GetCurrentProcess, GetProcAddress, LoadLibraryA, VirtualAlloc and CreateThread.
claim.interpretation: a.exe is a small (~9 KB) standalone PE staged in a user's Temp directory whose import set (VirtualAlloc + WriteProcessMemory + CreateThread + LoadLibraryA + GetProcAddress) is the canonical primitive sequence for allocating memory and injecting/executing shellcode in a process. It carries a non-vendor developer PDB path (c:\code\httppump\...\i.pdb) and no legitimate publisher metadata. This is consistent with a malicious code-injection loader/stager rather than legitimate software. Static strings alone do not prove it executed or identify its C2.
